entry.title: Re: How can we highlight New or Featured trainings more p...
entry.content:
Hi @RH1 

Yes. As far as I'm aware of, the classifications Featured, New, and Revised are only visible as a filter (in the Flagged section) through Enhanced Search. That is, these values are not displayed anywhere else.

Best regards,

entry.title: Re: Background jobs for vendor and document changes in fiori
entry.content:
Hello @Shama321 

Thank you for your question. In SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, transactions like S_ALR_87012089 (Vendor Changes) and S_ALR_87012293 (Document Changes) are generally not available, because the Public Cloud restricts classic GUI transactions

Refer to the SAP Help documentation below

  • Schedule Application Jobs → App ID: F1680.SAP Help Portal – Application Job Scheduling (S/4HANA Cloud)
  • SAP Help Portal – Configuration to Schedule an Application Job (S/4HANA Cloud)
  •  Some reporting gaps may be addressed in future Public Cloud releases. You can check the SAP Roadmap Explorer

Execute in Background for classic ALR reports like Vendor Changes and Document Changes is not enabled in Public Cloud

With kind regards

Chuma

entry.title: Guidance Required on Enabling DTO for ADSOs with Non-Key Time Characteristics in BW/4HANA
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

We have a requirement to enable DTO for certain ADSOs in our BW/4HANA system. However, we have encountered a few ADSOs where the time characteristics (e.g., CALDAY, FISCPER) are not part of the key fields.

Could you please provide guidance on how to enable DTO in such cases, especially when these ADSOs already contain data?

We would appreciate your input on the steps or actions required to proceed with the DTO setup in this scenario.

Thank you in advance for your support.

Best regards,
Shyamala

entry.title: SLD does not update the company list even though the SRGC table is correct
entry.content:
Hi, I'm facing a persistent issue with the System Landscape Directory (SLD) in SAP Business One.

When I log into the SAP B1 client and click on "Change Company", then hit "Refresh", the list of companies either disappears or shows outdated information. If I switch between the available SQL servers (SQL 2012 and SQL 2014), the list reappears, but it does not reflect recent changes (e.g., deleted databases still appear or new ones are missing).

I've already checked the SRGC table in the SBO-COMMON database, and it contains the correct and up-to-date company information. However, the SLD still shows outdated entries.

I also accessed the SLD through the web browser, and the issue is the same: it does not show the actual list of available databases.

I'm considering removing and re-registering the SQL server in the SLD, but I'm unsure whether this might affect any configuration or deployment.

Has anyone experienced this issue?

  • What file or process does SAP use to rebuild the company list from the SQL instance?

  • Is it safe to remove and re-add the server in the SLD?

  • How can I force the SLD to resync properly with SBO-COMMON?

Any guidance would be greatly appreciated.

entry.title: Re: Criterio al articulo 29 de la Ley del INFONAVIT
entry.content: Entonces, les sugiero ingresar un incidente para reportarlo a SAP.

entry.title: Re: EWM – Physical Inventory PI_UPLOAD transaction
entry.content:
Here is my email = Imrshek@gmail.com. if you can send it. Thanks.

And yes I tried downloading, but same file i cannot upload in pi_upload ....as it has many columns missing.

entry.title: Re: Special Stock Batch Determination for consignment iss...
entry.content:
Good Morning,

I think we should analyze this case point by point.

Regarding the first part:

"We are running production order against consignment sales as this is make to order as per the customer specifications. As production order is getting reserved against special stock for consignment fill up, we are unable to determine the batch, as the system considers only unrestricted stock."

Yes you are right, by default system is checking unrestricted stock during batch determination. In this case you would need to create your own procedure for special stock (create condition table where one of the fields would be special stock - SOBKZ).

For example key combination could look like:

- Order type/Plant/Special Stock

Of course you would also need to add this table to the to access sequence and finish the configuration (please let me know if you need a detailed information here).

And the second part of the case is that production is producing goods against consignment fill-up order (if misunderstood it please let me know). So after the production goods are goods receipt on stock with a special stock = E (customer order stock). So we have on stock material with specific batch assigned to customer stock. And we want to use the same batch during consignment issue process (consignment sales). In this case I think there should be any problem since during the shipment of customer order stock (special stock = E) to customer it will change the special stock type. At this moment special stock = O (consigment).

At this moment condition table that has been created previously should solve the problem with batch determination.

Plase let me know if you need any other information in this case.

Thank you and best regards

Dariusz Kalmarczyk

entry.title: Re: Criterio al articulo 29 de la Ley del INFONAVIT
entry.content: Gracias Hugo, ya tengo mi ticket con soporte SAP.

entry.title: Re: SAP BTP Learning Paths for Developers, Architects & More [2025 Updated]
entry.content: Thank you for the wonderful post!

entry.title: Options for multilingual content
entry.content:
Hello experts

We are looking for options to launch SCORM courses in multiple languages ​​for users. We want to improve the user experience when accessing e-learning courses available in multiple languages, so that:
- The user does not have to open multiple tabs in their browser.
- The user can complete the entire process (course selection, viewing, and navigation) from a single view or screen.

We want to achieve a fluid, simple, and centralized experience for multilingual users.

For example, some clients use subtitles within SCORM content and display them in the user's language.

Other clients create one item for each language and each SCORM in the corresponding language.

Have you had the opportunity to implement other methods for multilingual content?

Thank you.

entry.title: Error-Distribución de centros de costos
entry.content:
En ambiente preview, estoy tratando de editar o agregar un registro nuevo para el portlet de distribución de centros de costos y me genera el siguiente error:

errorId=bec40559-bb97-42ea-9cdf-13d9050a46b5, fingerprint=27fc5841e8f93d9f953050715ad17397d3d8858e, timestamp=2025-06-24T22:32:09.783+0000, errorMessage=Se produjo un error de aplicación. Vuelva a intentarlo más tarde o notifique a la asistencia técnica (comunique la información de error errorId=bec40559-bb97-42ea-9cdf-13d9050a46b5, timestamp=2025-06-24T22:32:09.783+0000 y la información de versión de la pantalla actual) si el problema sigue sucediendo.

entry.title: Re: Export Control Clarification Request for SAP S/4HANA...
entry.content: Dear Tsvetinka, Thank you for your guidance. I updated my P-user details to use only Latin characters as instructed, and I was able to proceed with activating the appliance successfully. Best regards, a5ymnk

entry.title: Re: How to connect from SAC to Azure SFTP server to load...
entry.content:
We found the solution:

Using SAC Agent installed on the Azure server (which is internally connected to its' SFTP server), which acts as a bridge between the SAC Tenants and the Azure SFTP server.

Thanks,

SP.

entry.title: Re: Custom MDF Analysis Dimension problem - Neuroinclusion
entry.content:
Hi @DariusSoreata - I don't think you understand how the MDF object and association with another MDF object works.  The association between Neuroinclusion and NeuroinclusionDetails object are stored in separate table/view.  The error is because you create a circular link between the two objects as shown on the following:

In Neuroinclusion MDF object setting, the external Code is of User data type so that means external code of Neuroinclusion object is storing User Sys ID value.  You can setup this field as Primary Person ID.  Next you need to link NeuroinclusionDetails object to Neuroinclusion object with the help of association table/view.  

Tips - you can use Event tab to have a look at table/view data.  If you search under Metadata Framework category, you will find Generic Relationship view that link Neuroinclusion with NeuroinclusionDetails object:

Based on the data, Destination object type is NeuroinclusionDetails object and Source object type is Neuroinclusion.  This means Source Row ID column is linked to Row ID in Neuroinclusion object and Destination Internal ID is linked to Internal ID of NeuroinclusionDetails object.

I've provided you with the tips, the rest you need to think how to implement this yourself. 

Note - If you need more detail support from SAP, you need to purchase service from SAP.  There is no free 1-1 support to help with the partner implementation.  Partners are expected to have the necessary knowledge to implement WFA on HANA and this forum only intended to provide guidance or basic support for errors/issues.  We don't do consulting for customization in this forum.

entry.title: Re: We cannot copy the number range RF_BELEG by subobject...
entry.content:
Thank you!!

And I want to copy the intervals by subobject. like Tr-cd:OBH1. 

another solution?

entry.title: Re: Error Log - Pay Comp Recurring Calc Columns
entry.content:
Hi @DariusSoreata - there is a Restricted Calculated Column tab which only SAP consultant can access because of security reason.  This tab is used to create more complex calculation using fields or calculated columns created in Table and Columns tab.  

In the template configuration, we have two restricted calculated columns to calculate Annual Salary and defined Salary Range dimension value.  These restricted calculated columns are using calculated columns created in Pay Comp Recurring table.  Since you remove most of the calculated fields in Pay Comp Recurring table, it breaks the processing of Annual Salary restricted calculated column.  See attached the full formula for the Annual Salary restricted calculated column.

My suggestion is to restore the configuration prior to deletion of calculated fields in Pay Comp Recurring table to fix it.

There is no free 1-1 support for partner implementation.  If you want more assistance, you need to purchase service from SAP store.

entry.title: Re: Criterio al articulo 29 de la Ley del INFONAVIT
entry.content: Hola Mayra! ¿me puedes enviar el número de incidente, por favor? Para darle seguimiento. Lo puedes enviar a mi correo en SAP: hu.rodriguez@sap.com

entry.title: Unable to Create Replication Model for SAP_COM_0009
entry.content:
I am trying to configure the integration between SAP IBP and SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition using the communication scenario SAP_COM_0009 (Product Integration).

However, I am not able to create a replication model, as the application “Manage Replication Models” is not available in my SAP S/4HANA Cloud system.

I would like to confirm the following:

Is the reason due to the required scope item not being included in the system?

How can I check and activate the necessary scope item?

Specifically, I suspect scope item 1YB – Data Replication using DRF might be required.

Is there any official help documentation or SAP Note that explains how to create a replication model for SAP_COM_0009?

Any guidance on how to enable the right scope item and complete the replication model setup would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you in advance!

The SAP help only recommends blocking tasks first in the data monitor and it is not PERSON_NAME.

 

 

Thanks


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

entry.title: Re: Unable to deply XPI Inspector with SUM
entry.content:
Hi Symon,

1. As per the SAP Note 2010715, during the SUM process, you may not deploy the XPI inspector.

2. Refer to the link to deploy the XPI inspector.

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-sap/undeploying-sca-sda-ear-war-files-on-j2ee-servers-on-netweaver-release-7-1/ba-p/13219513

Regards

SS

entry.title: Global CALM account access
entry.content:
Provide global account access for CALM for S user 

 

FYI: I have never received global account access or details.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Global CALM account access
entry.content:
Dear Customer,

 

Thanks for contacting SAP.

I will recommend you to go through solution provided under KBA 3365171 - How to get full Admin/IT Contact access for SAP Cloud ALM? 

For the situation: 

A. The existing SAP Cloud ALM Administrator is available:

Contact the SAP Cloud ALM Administrator and request that they follow KBA 3248116.

The instructions from the KBA will grant the following Administrator access:

  • "Global Account Administrator" role collection assigned for the SAP BTP cockpit Global account named "SAP Cloud ALM"or "SAP Cloud ALM - <ERP Cust Num>".

 

B: The existing SAP Cloud ALM Administrator is not available (left the company, extended leave, etc.)

Follow the instructions from KBA 3342350 - How to add an Administrator to the Global and/or Sub accounts for SAP Cloud ALM in the SAP BTP cockpit?.

In this KBA you will notice that you can now add a user as an Administrator in the SAP Cloud ALM BTP Global Account and SAP Cloud ALM BTP Sub-account directly via self-service in SAP for Me. Please review these KBAs for guidance:

        • 2669325 - How to add a user as a Global Account administrator for SAP Business Technology Platform
        • 3412209 - How to assign yourself to a subaccount as a Global Account admin?

Follow the steps in KBA 2991636 - "How to change the Main IT Contact/Technical Administrator for an SAP BTP Global Account" to ensure that the account executive updates the details with the new Admin information.

 

Please feel free to let me know when you have further inquiries regarding this KBA.

 

Best Regards,

Sunny

entry.title: Re: SAP TM Transportation Audit Display Details for Metrics

Moonjun

entry.title: How to map custom attributes and groups from IdP(OIDC) to a Fiori App via SAP CIS?
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

I have a Fiori application on SAP BTP using a proxied trust setup: BTP -> CIS -> AWS Cognito.

I have successfully configured the trust, and users can log in. The standard attributes (first name, last name, email) appear correctly in my application.

The Problem: I cannot get custom attributes and user groups from Cognito to appear in my app's user session.

What I Have Done:

  1. Cognito: I have confirmed that my Cognito ID Token contains the custom attributes. The audit log in CIS shows the payload it receives from Cognito includes:

    • "custom: country": "Australia"

    • "cognito: groups": ["BTP_Viewers"]

  2. SAP BTP (xs-security.json): I have declared that my application wants to receive these attributes:

{
  "xsappname": "sap-fiori-hello-world",
  "tenant-mode": "dedicated",
  "description": "Hello World SAP Fiori app with AWS Cognito authentication via CIS",
  "scopes": [
    {
      "name": "openid",
      "description": "Default scope for all authenticated users."
    }
  ],
  "attributes": [
    {
      "name": "given_name",
      "description": "User's First Name",
      "valueType": "string"
    },
    {
      "name": "family_name",
      "description": "User's Last Name",
      "valueType": "string"
    },
    {
      "name": "groups",
      "description": "User's Groups",
      "valueType": "string",
      "valueRequired": false
    },
    {
      "name": "custom_country",
      "description": "User's Country",
      "valueType": "string"
    }
  ],
  "oauth2-configuration": {
    "redirect-uris": [
      "https://*.*.hana.ondemand.com/**",
      "https://*.*.hana.ondemand.com/login/callback"
    ]
  }
}
  • SAP CIS: I have enabled Identity Federation ("Use Identity Authentication user store") on my Corporate Identity Provider settings for Cognito.

    The Result:

    When I check the /user-api/attributes endpoint in my Fiori app, I only see the standard attributes. The groups and custom_country attributes are missing.

{
  "firstname": "myfirstname",
  "lastname": "mylastname",
  "email": "my@email.com",
  "name": "my@email.com",
  "scopes": [
    "openid"
  ]
}

 

My Question:

Since the data is arriving at CIS from Cognito but not reaching my BTP application, what is the exact and final step required within SAP Cloud Identity Services to map these custom attributes (custom: country) and groups (cognito: groups) so they are included in the token sent to BTP?

I suspect I am missing a mapping on the application level within CIS, and I would appreciate help here.

Thank you

entry.title: Adobe Forms - Displaying nested loops
entry.content:
Hello Team,

I am working on an adobe form and the requirement is to display 3 nested loops on the form. Could you please help me with the steps to follow here. Attaching a screenshot of the requirement.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Adobe Forms - Displaying nested loops
entry.content:
Dear Customer,

Your question about how to create nested loop in the Adobe form can be found in the Adobe Livecycle designer online help.

You can find the chapter "how to create a nested table using subforms".

You may also approach to Adobe support for any further questions.

Best Regards

SAP Support

entry.title: Re: How to Force Close Outbound Delivery in SAP After Partial Picking
entry.content:
Hi @rob_ariaans ,

Thank you for taking the time to read my blog and for sharing your thoughtful feedback.

It's a great tip that complements the scenario I shared.

Additionally, by using the standard output type EK00 (Picking List) for application V4 (Shipping Spec. case), automatic picking can also be enabled - it helps default the picked quantity to the delivery quantity, offering another way to simplify the process.

Thanks again for enhancing the discussion!

Best regards,
Leelamohan

entry.title: Restricting Service Call Type dropdown values
entry.content:
Hello SAP FSM Experts,

I’m configuring the Service Call Details screen on the FSM mobile app (iOS/Android/Windows) and need to restrict certain Service Call Type values. Specifically:

  • Disallow or grey out ZSC3, ZSC4, and ZSC5

  • Allow only ZSC1 and ZSC2

Is it possible to apply this restriction purely via Screen Configuration? If so, could you please share the exact settings or expressions I should use?

Thank you in advance!

entry.title: Re: Help Booking SAP S/4HANA Asset Management Certification as a Student
entry.content: Hi, have you had two separate/verified email accounts linked to your SAP ID? I think this is one of the checks run before it launches into the screen/dashboard (as a separate platform) where you can book an exam. Good luck!

entry.title: Re: How to Use Vendor Sub-Ranges in SAP MM for Multi-Currency Procurement
entry.content:
Hi @Sharky ,

Thank you for taking the time to read my blog and for sharing your thoughtful feedback.

I agree that maintaining info records at the plant level, along with properly structured purchasing organizations, can offer a more automated and scalable solution for handling multi-currency scenarios.

The intent of my blog was to showcase an alternative approach based on a specific business case where vendor sub-ranges were leveraged to manage procurement differentiation due to regional pricing and currency variations across global subsidiaries. While it may not be the standard or most optimal approach in every scenario.

Your perspective adds great value, especially highlighting the benefits of using global purchasing organizations and contracts effectively. I’ll definitely consider including your point of view as an additional consideration for readers in a future update or follow-up post.

Thanks again for contributing to the discussion!

Best regards,
Leelamohan

entry.title: Re: 公有云系统中，量差与价差的核算未能分别体现，全部核算到价差中，没有体现量差，如何处置？
entry.content:
Hi @lemon-xue,

您试了F4254（订单成本明细 - 基于事件）这个app吗？在右方页面cost details（成本明细）页签，可以查看生产工单的各种差异，如由于投料数量、投料价格变动的差异，及其按总账科目的细分。这些都是采用的标准价格。

这里的总差异会在CKM3（物料价格分析）中主视图的“生产”节点下汇总为一行“订单结算”的价格差异列，并细分为各成本组件。此外，会另外显示几行由于报工等累计的标准费率与实际费率差异。CKM3还会在“评估数量结构”中显示生产该物料的投料和报工的各项价差。

如果这些不能满足您的需求，能否麻烦您创建客户需求条目https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/campaign/1169，详细说明一下您的需求？谢谢！

Grace

entry.title: SmartEdit enhancements in Commerce Cloud 2025 Q2 release
entry.content:
Several improvements are introduced to SmartEdit in the SAP Commerce Cloud release 2211.41.

Enhanced Pages List Management in SmartEdit: A Customizable User-Friendly Experience

We introduced several improvements to our SmartEdit Pages list functionality, designed to give content managers and editors’ more control and flexibility while improving efficiency. These updates focus on customization, sorting, and persistence of settings for a smoother workflow.

Key Improvements

1. Customizable Columns

• Add Predefined Attribute Columns or Hide Columns: Need an extra column for a specific attribute? Simply add it from a list of predefined options; also Users can now hide columns they don’t need.

Users now have complete control over which columns appear in their Pages list view by simply toggle column visibility, which create a clean, focused interface showing only the data you need. This is particularly valuable for users who need to focus on specific page attributes during their workflow.

Note columns (attributes) are predefined. If you want to show other attributes, you need to do customization implementation.

• Column Reordering: Rearrange column positions for better visibility. Rearrange column by Move to Top, Move Up, Move Down or Move to Bottom.

• Adjust Column Widths: Resize columns intuitively by dragging their borders to create your ideal layout

2. Adjustable Pagination

• Choose from multiple size options (10, 20, 50, or 100 items per page of Pages list view)

• Larger sizes option benefit users to work with less pagination, while smaller sizes improve performance on slower connections.

• Default setting of 20 items provides a balance.

3. Enhanced Sorting Capabilities

• Most columns now support ascending/descending sorting

• Default sort applies to "Last Modified" column in descending order (showing most recent changes first)

• Certain columns are excluded from sorting due to their complex nature:

o Multi-language fields (Page Type, Page Description, Title)

o Display Rules (Restrictions)

o Status indicators

o Action columns

4. Browser-Based Memory

All Page List configurations are automatically saved to browser local storage, Includes:

o Column visibility selections

o Column order preferences

o Active sorting parameters

o Chosen page size

No more repetitive configuration required when using the same browser - your preferred layout loads automatically.

Why These Changes Matter

These improvements ensure a more personalized and efficient experience when managing pages in the SmartEdit. By allowing users to tailor the Page List to their needs and remembering their preferences, we reduce repetitive adjustments and streamline content management.

These enhancements translate to tangible benefits for CMS users:

1. Time Savings:

o Eliminates repetitive configuration tasks

o Reduces time spent searching for relevant information

2. Improved Accuracy & Productivity:

o Custom views minimize distractions from irrelevant data

o Persistent sorting ensures consistent information hierarchy

o Tailored interfaces match individual working styles

3. Reduced Training Needs:

o Intuitive customization lowers learning curve

 

Enhanced Preview in SmartEdit: Preview by User Groups

We enhanced SmartEdit Preview functionality. The User Group-Based Preview designed to give you (SmartEdit user, e.g. Content Manager, Content Editor) more control and flexibility to preview changes and settings that you have made to your contents. This allows you to view pages by integrating user group context alongside existing contexts (content catalog version, time, language, product catalog version).

Key improvement

• Allow SmartEdit user to select a user group from a dropdown list during preview setup.

• Allow simulating users belonging to multiple groups. Users can select multiple (up to 10) user groups from a dropdown list during preview setup.

• The CMS applies the selected user group(s) as preview context to simulate the view of the website. For example, if simulating multiple user groups e.g. user groups A and B as preview context, SmartEdit user would be able to preview contents with user group A restriction, user Group B restriction , ‘user group A or user group B’ restriction and ‘user group A and user group B’ restriction.

Note that if SmartEdit user previews page after simulating storefront user sign in , preview by user group does NOT impact user data of the signed in storefront user.

Why These Changes Matter

Content restricted to the selected user group(s) is displayed or hidden accordingly in SmartEdit by User Group preview.

Use Cases

• Content Validation for Targeted Audiences. Example: A marketing team previews a campaign page to ensure "Premium Members" see exclusive offers, while "Guests" see a generic version.

• Training and Demos . Example: Onboarding new CMS users by demonstrating how different user groups experience the site. 

 

Try it out and let us know what you think! Which feature do you find most useful?

entry.title: Re: Reporting on Manual Overall Rating
entry.content: Hi Geoff, have you ever resolved the issue. We also have the same requirement but the manual overall form rating seems can not be extracted so far.

entry.title: Re: Output tables in Kmeans
entry.content:
Hi Francesco,

The free version of K-Means does not have any usage restrictions. This issue might be related to the dataset.

Could you provide more detail information about data set? I would help us better analyze this issue.

B&R,

Michael

entry.title: Re: Actual Activity Rate Calculation without using Materi...
entry.content:
HI Marvin,

1. Thanks for confirming that there is currently no functionality in S4 Public cloud which is equivalent of CON2 in On-prem/Private Cloud.
- This implies that the "Activity Price variances" arising due to difference between planned and Actual activity prices can not be allocated to the production order, unless the Actual Costing is used.

2. Regarding other point in your Response: "You can use the actual activity prices to revaluate the allocations between cost centers with transaction/app KSII."
- I believe the App "Actual Cost Rate Calculation" becomes available for actual activity price calculation only when we activate 33Q scope item. So, not sure how can we still calculate the actual activity prices without having 33Q activated.
- And even if we activate 33Q only to use the KSII functionality (and not to use actual costing), then can you please please elaborate on how to "Revaluate the Allocations between Cost Centers" to ensure that the under-absorbed expenses at the production cost centers get allocated to the PRD accounts?

Karan

entry.title: Re: Down Payment Invoices (Advance Payment Invoices) in France
entry.content: Then, as I said in my above post, you can raise a ticket with SAP citing the above SAP notes is not meeting your requirement. They will suggest you in the right direction

entry.title: Story Report
entry.content:
Hello,

While proxying some users, I am able to get data in story reports, but for others, there is no output while proxying. Could you please advise?

Br!

entry.title: Re: Introductory Guide to Provisioning of SAP Business Data Cloud
entry.content:
@PraveenJelli 
Hello,
You can navigate to the SAP Discovery Center to understand the sizing of the Datasphere tenants. The CU required will be dependent on several factors, as per your use cases, which you can find in the calculator.
SAC, you can select the number of licenses you require, and CU will be adjusted and consume that many CU accordingly.
*CU = Capacity Units

entry.title: Re: ABAP Cloud Developer Trial 2022 Available Now
entry.content:
Hi Roman, 

It seems that your container is not fully started. Have a look at the main memory usage, just below 800MB! Try to restart the container. If the container runs correctly the memory usage is approximately above 12 GB...

Regards

entry.title: Re: Create just Purchase Requisition for Stock Material f...
entry.content:
See below SAP's explanation

With the item category "L", system first generates Reservation

entry.title: Re: BRFplus Sales Document Item Categories for Consignment
entry.content:
Dear Customer,

Yes. These sales document types are not supported in the BRF+ solution. Please feel free to raise requirement to SAP to get them supported. Thanks! 

Best regards,

Yong

entry.title: Re: I can not open Service Binding in Eclipse
entry.content: @Ndubuisi_PALMER According to our Rules of Engagement, I have added the "GenAI was used to help generate this content" statement to your answer, as the content has been AI-generated. Transparency is key when it comes to AI, we therefore require that AI-generated content is marked as such: "If you want to publish content that was helped/created by GenAI, you must add the user tag GenAI Assisted Content. For content where it's not possible to add a user tag (e.g., answers), you must include this statement: "GenAI was used to help generate this content." Thank You!

entry.title: Maintain Ship-to-party parameter in SAP PO Template
entry.content:
Hi，

I want to change my company's ship-to-party of  PO Print template, does everybody knows where can I search this parameter? Use which App? It's the address of my company, not customer.

entry.title: error while using DTW
entry.content:
Dear All,

I want update column UDF with Name: U_MonthSchool of Journal Entry(OJDT). However, when import DTW error: Invalid valid value '' in Enum ‘BoYesNoEnum’, the valid values are ‘N’, ‘Y’.
Please help me ! Thanks you a lot.

entry.title: Re: Issue with deleted Purchase Requisition still generat...
entry.content: Check whether you can run the report Z_CORRECT_RESB_ENMNG_ENWRT after carrying out code corrections as recommended in SAP note 1125168 to clear the entry from RESB-KZEAR

entry.title: Re: Ariba : VIM Invoice attachment is not showing in /n/o...
entry.content: The issue is due to the support pack. Kindly check your support for this kind of issue

entry.title: Cost element category 90 in S4 - alternative approaches
entry.content:
Hello 

i understand cost element category 90 in public cloud is discontinued and in private cloud it is not recommended but still supported. In public cloud the alternative seems to be CE Type 1 if needed margin analysis characteristics are not automatically populated (e.g as with WIP). In our case we need to post MA characteristics on regular accrual accounts so need the BS account as account assignment. I tried to open BS accounts as type 1 in our private cloud system: this requires me to set a balance carry forward account then which makes no sense plus also does not allow OI managment. 

So replacing CE Type 90 with 1 in a private cloud environment does not seem to be the solution. What else can i do to avoid CE Type 90?

entry.title: Re: Down payment in sales order
entry.content: Hi Liz, are going to create sales order manually? If yes, it's not possible to split the values without knowing the values of each of the line items. What we do in our project, we have created a custom FM to create a SO, and in that FM you enter the % of downpayment you want specific for each line item along with the Header Billing plan.

entry.title: Re: Story Report
entry.content:
Hello there

Could you please check while proxy as the end user who are not be able to view the data in story report has below RBP permission settings.

3213963 - How to find what permissions are missing for the users in the Story report - People Analytics - SAP for Me

For Employee Central data, users must have permission for the portlets as well (e.g. employmentInfo fields requires permission for 'Job Information Actions' (View Current and View History) and 'Employment Details MSS' and for personalInfo / personInfo it is also required the 'Personal Information Actions' (View Current and View History)).

For non-EC objects (e.g. user_information_sac), the permission can vary (e.g. Data Access). Please refer Permissions Required for Using Story Reports | SAP Help Portal.

Hope helps here.

Thanks!

entry.title: Re: SLD, License and Extension Manager Error Installing S...
entry.content:
Your installed version FP2202 is too low for a direct upgrade. You need to upgrade to SAP B1 FP2208 before you can upgrade to SAP B1 SP2502.

Please refer to SAP Business One Upgrade Strategy Overview

entry.title: Re: S/4HANA Embedded Extended Warehouse Management (EWM) Overview
entry.content:
Nice Blog

Best Regards,

Anirudh

entry.title: Sudden wrong results in app behaviour for previous well working formulas in settings and components
entry.content:
Hello

@timokapanen 

With present build engine 4.15.23, I noticed on my two iOS apps compiling mistakes appearing suddenly together:

1. Formula not working any more:

I use lay out programming with formula using an app variable named DisplayShift at launch of the app with calculated value as  IF(IS_EQUAL(systemVars.formFactor,"phone"),35,0)

and using it in each pages nested in a global container lay out for left spacing as 

IF(CONTAINS(systemVars.orientation,"landscape_left"),appVars.DisplayShift,0)

along with right spacing

IF(CONTAINS(systemVars.orientation,"landscape_right"),appVars.DisplayShift,0)

The lay out is no more working. It was working well before.

if I force the value as 35 in the first formula it is then working again. so the bug is in the compiling (building) of the first formula: IF(IS_EQUAL(systemVars.formFactor,"phone"),35,0)

Before was ok in previous releases. I had changed nothing.

2. At app launch, the component 'preload audio for playback' with local path in a formula with a variable containing the path was working well previously, now no more working. I had to replace with the direct path information instead recalling from the app variable and then it worked again.

It seems the building engine has issue when two levels of calculation are inside a formula. Or maybe the building engine is newly more sentive (unduly) to format of data?

Anyway, it should not suddenly stop working when it worked before.

Thank you for checking.

Best regards

Chantal Chi

entry.title: Re: SAP Analytics Cloud - Level Hierarchy - Help please
entry.content:
Hello.

The root cause is that you have no values (empty) in Region and Sub-Region properties for Unassigned member in your dimension. table.

 

When you created new Dimension "Region_0" in your model, with default member in in its hierarchy as "Unassigned", this member will by default be populated in the story. Therefore when you try to expand this hierarchy, you will see the values of other levels assigned for this member in the dimension.

Eg.

 

I hope this helps.

With best regards,

Slava

entry.title: Integrating Microsoft Services with SAP Build Process Automation using Microsoft 365 SDK & Graph API
entry.content:
Disclaimer: The insights shared in this blog are based on my personal observations and learning. They may not apply universally, and I encourage readers to conduct their own research and make an informed decision before using this content for productive use.

 

Introduction

In the modern digital workplace, automation is no longer a luxury—it's a necessity. Businesses are continuously looking for ways to connect systems, eliminate manual effort, and accelerate decision-making. SAP Build Process Automation offers a powerful platform for automating business workflows, while Microsoft 365 services—like Outlook, SharePoint, OneDrive, and Excel—are deeply embedded in everyday enterprise operations.

This blog explores how to securely integrate SAP Build Process Automation with Microsoft Entra ID (formerly Azure AD), enabling direct access to Microsoft 365 services through either the Microsoft 365 SDK or Microsoft Graph API. This integration opens up automation possibilities across a wide range of business functions—from reading and sending emails, to accessing SharePoint documents, managing Excel files, and interacting with OneDrive storage.

Business Challenge: Automating Cross-Platform Enterprise Processes

Enterprise workflows often span multiple systems and platforms—SAP for business logic, and Microsoft 365 for communication, document management, and collaboration. However, bridging these platforms typically requires manual data transfers, file handling, and task coordination. These repetitive, error-prone activities consume valuable time and create bottlenecks in business processes.

Key challenges include:

  • Limited out-of-the-box automation SDK for Microsoft services.
  • Security and compliance risks due to manual authentication and permission handling.
  • Lack of centralized access to documents, emails, spreadsheets, and collaboration data from within automation tools.

With this integration, organizations can:

  • Automate email operations without outlook.
  • Interact with SharePoint and OneDrive to manage and retrieve files.
  • Automate Excel data processing and reporting.
  • Ensure secure and controlled access using Microsoft Entra ID authentication.

By embedding Microsoft 365 services directly into SAP Build Process Automation, businesses can streamline end-to-end workflows across systems, enhance productivity, and reduce IT overhead.

 

Azure App Registration in Microsoft Entra ID

Log in to the Azure Portal:

Navigate to https://portal.azure.com.

Open Microsoft Entra ID under Azure Services.

Image 1

From the left navigation pane, click on App registrations and then New registration.

Image 2

Image 3

Provide a Name for the app, choose the appropriate Supported account types, and set the Redirect URI type to Web.
Enter the following URI:
https://login.microsoftonline.com/common/oauth2/nativeclient.

Image 4

After registration:

Copy the Tenant ID and Client ID.

Generate a Client Secret by going to Certificates & Secrets.

These credentials are required for both Microsoft 365 SDK and Graph API integrations.

Image 5

Image 6

Method 1: Integrate Using Microsoft 365 SDK

Open SAP Build Process Automation Lobby and navigate to Control Tower > External Authentication.

Image 7

Click Create New Authentication, select Microsoft 365, and enter:

Tenant ID, Client ID, Client Secret

Choose the required Scopes based on your use case.

Image 8

Open the Desktop Agent on your local machine.

Image 9

Now Navigate to Settings → External Authentication → Select the Microsoft Authentication entry and Sign in using your user or technical account.

Image 10

Image 11

Image 12

If you're a Global Admin, you can check Consent on behalf of your organization during the first login prompt.

Note: Individual users can also provide consent for their own accounts.

Image 13

Set this authentication entry as your Default Connection in the Desktop Agent.

Image 14

In your Process Automation Project:

Add the Microsoft 365 Cloud SDK as a dependency.

Image 15

Use built-in activities such as:

Search Emails (e.g., filter by folder: Inbox or by subject)

Send Email (e.g., specify recipient, subject, and body)

Image 16

Upon execution, you can validate:

Emails fetched from the inbox

Outgoing emails successfully delivered to recipients

Image 17

Image 18

Image 19

Image 20

Image 21

Method 2: Integrate Using Microsoft Graph API

For more advanced or customized use cases, you can use the Microsoft Graph API to interact with Microsoft services programmatically.

Permissions Setup:

Review and assign required permissions from Microsoft Graph API Reference docs.

Use Graph Explorer to test API calls and review responses.

If permissions require admin approval, click Grant admin consent for Default Directory.

 

Image 22

Image 23

Image 24

Image 25

Image 26

Image 27

Image 28

Generate Access Token:

Use your Tenant ID, Client ID, and Client Secret to form the authentication request.

Refer to standard OAuth 2.0 token generation flow (as shown in Image 29).

Image 29

Image 30

Call the Graph API:

Use the obtained Access Token to make API calls to endpoints such as:
https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/users/{UserID}/mailFolders('Inbox')/messages

Fetch email data or other resources as needed (refer to Image 32 for sample response).

Image 31

Image 32

Conclusion: Unified Automation Across SAP and Microsoft Ecosystems

Integrating SAP Build Process Automation with Microsoft Entra ID and Microsoft 365 services delivers a robust foundation for enterprise-grade automation. Whether using the Microsoft 365 SDK for a plug-and-play experience, or leveraging Microsoft Graph API for advanced customization, this approach allows seamless access to:

  • Outlook – for reading, searching, and sending emails
  • SharePoint & OneDrive – for retrieving, storing, and managing documents
  • Excel – for accessing and automating spreadsheet-based operations
  • Teams & Calendar (optional) – for integrating communication and scheduling meetings

Security is built-in through OAuth-based authentication and consent management, ensuring compliance while enabling automation at scale.

By centralizing access and automating repetitive actions across Microsoft and SAP platforms, businesses reduce complexity, minimize errors, and free up valuable resources. This is more than a technical integration—it's a strategic enabler for digital transformation.

entry.title: Re: How to map custom attributes and groups from IdP(OIDC) to a Fiori App via SAP CIS?

entry.title: Re: Require Current Date VK11 Pricing
entry.content: Yes it does. this current date price master is required for a different server outside of SAP. I cant send this report to that server directly from VK13. I need to create a separate report, due to which i need that table-field of Validity period From/On.

entry.title: Two-Tier Refurbishment with Service
entry.content:
Introduction:

This blog post will provide the detailed process on Two-Tier ERP solution for the process of Equipment Refurbishment with sales & services integration in SAP S/4HANA Cloud from headquarters to subsidiary which run on two different instances of SAP

There are two scenarios-One is, where the equipment is owned by headquarters and is leased out to the subsidiary for usage and maintenance, is sent to Headquarters in case of refurbishment. Second scenario is where a customer has rented the equipment, and servicing

happens at the subsidiary and in case the equipment needs to be refurbished it is sent to headquarters for refurbishment. This scenario supports organizations running on Two-Tier setup to have better coverage on Refurbishment process between Tier 1 and Tier 2 systems.

Overview:

In an ever-evolving business model customers require product or equipment for their manufacturing activities for specific machine manufactures for long term on rent /Lease basis to avoid capital expenditure.  Hence OEMs or headquarters provide equipment on rental basis for a particular lease duration. Since the machine is owned by the headquarter periodic maintenance must be ensured.

Scenario 1:

Headquarters leases out a high value equipment to the subsidiary on lease basis for a particular time with subsidiary paying monthly rents and spending on equipment maintenance. There is an equivalent lease-in contract at the subsidiary with automatic creation of asset master and manual creation of Equipment master. During Asset maintenance in case the equipment needs refurbishment, it is sent to headquarters for Refurbishment

Scenario 2:

To understand better, customer places a contract with the subsidiary for an equipment. Equipment is delivered to the customer as a consignment and updated in master as equipment at customer location. The parameters or indicators are maintained in the equipment master. The subsidiary is informed of the repair or service required with the customer and equipment details. Monthly rentals are charged against the contracts for the period equipment is at customer location. Between the customer and subsidiary, vendor invoicing is done for the service or repair carried out on the equipment. The subsidiary decides if the equipment needs repair or refurbishment in during annual equipment maintenance through service order. In case of repair, in-house repair is carried out at subsidiary and in case of refurbishment, the damaged equipment is sent to headquarters for Refurbishment with headquarters sending the replaced equipment back to customer via subsidiary.


Business Enterprises implemented with Two-Tier ERP scenario involving Equipment refurbishment and setup wherein the subsidiary runs on SAP S/4HANA Cloud, public edition acting as Tier 2 and the Headquarter running on any deployments such as SAP S/4HANA Cloud, private edition, SAP S/4HANA, or SAP ECC or any other ERP system acting as Tier 1 which can cater to refurbishment of Leased/Rented equipment.

SAP S/4HANA Cloud, public edition system plans the refurbishment schedule for the equipment and notifies the Headquarter whenever the equipment is due for refurbishment. The Headquarter on the other hand refurbishes the equipment.


Two-Tier Solution:

Scenario 1

To achieve the above s requirement, SAP’s Two -Tier ERP Solution provides direct integration between SAP S/4HANA Cloud, public edition system at the subsidiary and SAP S/4HANA / SAP S/4HANA Cloud public edition / SAP ECC/other ERP system at the Headquarter.

Let’s see the process flow,

Two- Tier Refurbishment : Scenario 1

 

Glossary

Tier-2

  • An equivalent leased in contract is created at subsidiary along with an asset master. An equipment created manually referencing to the asset.
  • In due course if the maintenance team sees a need that requires the equipment to be refurbished a maintenance order for refurbishment is created at subsidiary.
  • This maintenance order will create an event. This event is sent to Event Mesh and is replicated to Tier 1. The data replication is done by capturing the event using an AMQP adapter in an integration flow and then replicating the maintenance order to Tier 1 via an OData service to create the Refurbishment order
  • At subsidiary, with reference to maintenance order a subcontracting Purchase requisition and purchase order is created for replacement material and material to be refurbished as a component
  • A transfer posting is done to transfer the damaged material to Vendor stock
  • On Goods Receipt of Purchase order will move the damaged material out of vendor stock and receipt of replacement material
  • The lease on damaged material can be ended and lease can be created for replacement material.
  • The maintenance order will be completed and settled.


Tier-1

Let’s see here the activities at Tier 1 system which is notified of the equipment that is due for refurbishment.

  • Tier 1 system receives the notification with maintenance order that the damaged material has to be refurbished
  • Now, a refurbishment order is created by the Headquarters to carry out the in house refurbishment process for the equipment.
  • Refurbishment process in carried out and refurbished material is maintained at the stock.


This way we can cater to refurbishment services provided by Headquarters for the Leased equipment with Two-Tier scenario.

Scenario 2:

In this scenario we have a rented equipment at customers location and the servicing happens at subsidiary that is on SAP S/4HANA Cloud, public edition and refurbishment happens at headquarters which is on SAP S/4HANA Cloud, private edition.

Two-Tier Refurbishment : Scenario 2

Tier 2

  • Customer places an order and rental contract is created with the material, contract duration and monthly rental to be paid as a periodic plan.
  • Consignment Fill-up order is created against the rental contract. Delivery Document is created for shipping the material to the customer. During PGI serial number is added.
  • Post PGI, equipment status for the serial number is updated to customer location with the customer details.
  • An annual maintenance plan is created that creates the periodic service orders
  • For servicing of the equipment through consignment pick up the equipment is moved to the subsidiary and in case of repair a repair order is created and if it needs a refurbishment, a maintenance order for refurbishment is created.
  • This maintenance order will create an event. This event is sent to Event Mesh and is replicated to Tier 1. The data replication is done by capturing the event using an AMQP adapter in an integration flow and then replicating the maintenance order to Tier 1 via an OData service to create the Refurbishment order
  • At subsidiary, with reference to maintenance order a subcontracting Purchase requisition and purchase order is created for replacement material and material to be refurbished as a component
  • A transfer posting is done to transfer the damaged material to Vendor stock
  • On Goods Receipt of Purchase order will move the damaged material out of vendor stock and receipt of replacement material
  • This replacement material will be sent back to the Customer via consignment return with respect to service order which was created earlier.
  • With respect to replaced material a new rental contract will be created and the rental contract for damaged material will be terminated.

Tier-1

Let’s see here the activities at Tier 1 system which is notified of the equipment that is due for refurbishment.

  • Tier 1 system receives the notification with maintenance order that the damaged material must be refurbished
  • Now, a refurbishment order is created by the Headquarters to carry out the in-house refurbishment process for the equipment.
  • Refurbishment process in carried out and refurbished material is maintained at the stock.

Business Benefits

When implementing Equipment Refurbishment as a process across a Two-Tier ERP architecture, especially with sales & services integration between headquarters and a subsidiary running on different ERP instances, there are several strategic and operational benefits.

  • End-to-End Process Visibility: Integration between HQ and subsidiary ERPs ensures real-time tracking of equipment
  • Optimized Refurbishment Workflow: Refurbishment requests can be initiated at the subsidiary (where equipment is handled) and approved/tracked by HQ.
  • Seamless Sales & Service Integration
  • Process Automation & Reduced Manual Errors: With API /Advanced event mesh technology data is exchanged automatically:
  • Reduces manual data entry and errors common in disconnected systems.
  • Efficient Inventory & Logistics Coordination: Minimizes downtime and maximizes refurbishment throughput.

Thanks for reading this blog post, hopefully the blog post was informative.

Two-Tier ERP Accelerators can be downloaded from best practice explorer 

Best Practice Explorer

entry.title: Re: How to Force Close Outbound Delivery in SAP After Partial Picking
entry.content: EK00 is for IM picking whereas the scenario in your blog is WM-relevant.

entry.title: Re: 1099 Reports(US Withholding tax)
entry.content: Good doc

entry.title: Re: Cannot Generate Handling Unit Using 311 to move stora...
entry.content:
Hi Melani,

        In S/4HANA public cloud, automatic packing with a delivery for a 311 movement is not possible because the delivery type DIG2 has no UPB profile.

        And auto packing is also not supported in synchronous goods movement of transfer posting in MIGO. 

Best regards,

Minghui

entry.title: Re: Require Current Date VK11 Pricing
entry.content: You can mention SYSDATUM in the FS which is nothing but system date and also current date.  Also the field DATAM is stored in Structure RV130 and not in table.  Of course, your ABAP team can add the required logic to check this.

entry.title: Securing the Future: Designing Scalable Access Control Systems with Bluetooth and Cloud Integration
entry.content:
Hello,
Dive into the challenges and considerations of developing modern access control systems for small businesses like clinics. Cover microcontroller selection, secure Bluetooth pairing protocols, and planning for future needs such as staff authentication, cloud-based logging, and remote management. dogsounds app


Best Regards,
Amy Cross

However, I'm unable to find this package in the system, despite following all the steps correctly. Could you please guide me on how to access or add this package?

entry.title: Re: Require Current Date VK11 Pricing
entry.content: Thank you so much

entry.title: Re: Adding localized Best Practices at SAP Signavio Proce...
entry.content: Good day Mauricio,

entry.title: Re: How to map custom attributes and groups from IdP(OIDC...
entry.content:
Try to add a reference to user_attributes in your xs-security.json foreign scope references:

    "foreign-scope-references": [
        "user_attributes"
    ]

I have a sample application showing Corporate IdP attributes in a CF application here: https://github.com/willempardaens/JWTUserAttributes/blob/main/xs-security.json#L112-L114

entry.title: Re: Adding localized Best Practices at SAP Signavio Proce...
entry.content: Good day Mauricio...we acknowledge your suggestion and have forwarded it to the respective team for consideration. Thank you. KantriceD

entry.title: Re: SAP CPQ - Cart -> Product cannot be configured unless...
entry.content:
Yes, this is a valid approach. I tried to create a quote via REST API (postman) and edited it afterwards with directly accessing it via url like mentioned in the documentation you provided.

But this feels a little complicated and much more effort from the caller application then from SAP CPQ (the caller application is not maintained by me).

As i wanted to have the problem solved within CPQ I implemented a responsive ui template which checks if there are url parameters added to the cart request.
If provided, I call the REST APIs to create a quote from the CPQ Cart UI myself, (same when done by hitting the new project/quote button in the cpq ui) then continue to preset fields. The authorization is already done. Problem solved.

entry.title: Re: Simplify how users find and share data: new AI features in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition (250
entry.content:
Dear @ConradBernal,

Thank you for sharing the detailed information. I have a quick question regarding the offering. Based on the details provided, are we required to accept the Limited Promotion Terms document and submit it in order to gain access to the new features?

Additionally, once we complete that step, will the features be available free of charge until July 31, 2025, for exploration purposes? After this promotional period, could you please clarify the process going forward? Will SAP begin charging for the continued use of these services?

Looking forward to your clarification.

“How can I try this out?”  

We’re very excited to get these features in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition out to you! Try them out, see how they can help you find, filter, and share information.

To get started, you must agree to the limited promotion terms document to get access to the features. Once you've done that, you're ready to go! 

Jump into one of your SAP-delivered list report applications to try out AI-assisted easy filter. 

Thank you!

entry.title: The standard doC option Vs CustOnTemp with SUM 2.0 SP23
entry.content: With the release of SUM 2.0 SP23, will the standard doC option continue to be available woth  CustOnTemp?

entry.title: Re: Restricting Service Call Type dropdown values
entry.content:
Good morning Minhas,

as I'm not sure about the scope of your requirement, I can only share some basic guidelines for you. It's not possible to 'grey out' specific service call types via the screen configuration. What you could do though is implement a validation expression:
https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_FIELD_SERVICE_MANAGEMENT/fsm_admin/field-settings.html
Alternatively you could set up a business rule that validates the service call type on sync and informs the user that he is not allowed to create service calls of this specific type:
https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_FIELD_SERVICE_MANAGEMENT/fsm_admin/validate.html

Please note however, that in the former approach, there might be slight differences in the UI for the different mobile plattforms.

Have a nice day ahead!

Best regards

Dmitry

entry.title: Re: How Official Document Numbering helps to fulfill the Polish KSeF Requirements in S4HANA Public C
entry.content: Hello, I'm also looking a solution how to store KSeF related information in SAP S/HANA on-premise system when using BTP and third party operator for sending Billing documents in Poland?

entry.title: Re: SAP SAC Intgerated Financial Planning Calculating pro...
entry.content:
Hi @SANA11 

First, verify if there is any data within the RL() scope shown below.

  • RESULTLOOKUP([d/Measures] = "QUANTITY", [d/Version] = "public.Actual", [d/Date] = PREVIOUS(12))
  • RESULTLOOKUP([d/Measures] = "AMOUNT", [d/Version] = "public.Actual", [d/Date] = PREVIOUS(12))

Second, check the dimension member IDs for the two RL() scopes above.

  • The member IDs of all other dimensions must match except for the Measure, Date, and IFP_GLACCOUNT members for the RL()/RL() calculation to produce a result.

The tracing feature in Advanced Formulas can be helpful for verifying the data.

 

Regards,

Moonjun

entry.title: Re: Cost planning integration between Controlling and Finance table ACDOCP
entry.content:
Hi All, 

Anybody got resolution for the issue of transferring balance of cost element category 42 ?

Thanks

entry.title: Re: Wrong behaviour of cl_gui_alv_grid or wrong usage?
entry.content:
Hi,

1) Why should the selection of a value "wrong" by F4 be reported as error? F4 is supposed to deliver only valid values in my opinion.
As a user of the application, I would be surprised and maybe even upset if a value help offers invalid data to me.

2) I found https://abapinho.com/en/2017/09/alv-grid-built-in-error-protocol/
go_grid->check_changed_data before save should report the error.

Referring to the header question, So, I m on the side "wrong usage"  🙂

Regards

Zeno

we are expecting as below,

Thanks 

Kavitha D

entry.title: Re: OVP Cards with Mobile and Tablet

entry.title: Re: SAP SAC Intgerated Financial Planning Calculating pro...
entry.content:
@MoonJuncan you please elaborate the point Second, check the dimension member IDs for the two RL() scopes above. The member IDs of all other dimensions must match except for the Measure, Date, and IFP_GLACCOUNT members for the RL()/RL() calculation to produce a result.

 

as the complete code is this CONFIG.HIERARCHY.INCLUDE_MEMBERS_NOT_IN_HIERARCHY = [d/SALES_ALL_COMPANY_CODE], [d/SAP__FI_IFP_GLACCOUNT], [d/SAP_ALL_PLANT], [d/SALES_ALL_PRODUCT], [d/SALES_ALL_CUSTOMER], [d/SALES_ALL_PROFITCENTER], [d/SALES_FI_IFP_FIXVARIABLE], [d/SALES_FI_IFP_PRTNRCOSTCENTE], [d/SALES_ALL_FUNCTIONALAREA], [d/SALES_FI_IFP_QUANTITY_UNIT] //----------------------------------------------------------------------------------- //The data region against which the formulas will be executed. //----------------------------------------------------------------------------------- MEMBERSET [d/Date] = [d/Version].[p/Start_date] TO [d/Version].[p/End_Date] MEMBERSET [d/SALES_FI_IFP_FIXVARIABLE] = "#" VARIABLEMEMBER #AMOUNT OF [d/Measures] VARIABLEMEMBER #DATE OF [d/Date] VARIABLEMEMBER #QUANTITY OF [d/Measures] //----------------------------------------------------------------------------------- //Calculation and Data Writing IF [d/SAP__FI_IFP_GLACCOUNT] = BASEMEMBER([d/SAP__FI_IFP_GLACCOUNT], "22") THEN // Gross Revenue DATA([d/Measures] = #QUANTITY,[d/SAP__FI_IFP_GLACCOUNT] = "#",[d/Date] = #DATE) = RESULTLOOKUP([d/Measures] = "QUANTITY", [d/Version] = "public.Actual", [d/Date] = PREVIOUS(12)) *-1 ENDIF IF [d/SAP__FI_IFP_GLACCOUNT] = BASEMEMBER([d/SAP__FI_IFP_GLACCOUNT], "21") THEN // COGS DATA([d/Measures] = #AMOUNT, [d/Date] = #DATE) = RESULTLOOKUP([d/Measures] = "AMOUNT", [d/Version] = "public.Actual", [d/Date] = PREVIOUS(12)) ENDIF //IF [d/SAP__FI_IFP_GLACCOUNT] = BASEMEMBER([d/SAP__FI_IFP_GLACCOUNT], "21") THEN // COGS DATA([d/Measures] = "PRICE") = RESULTLOOKUP([d/Measures] = #AMOUNT, [d/Date] = #DATE)/ RESULTLOOKUP([d/Measures] = #QUANTITY, [d/Date] = #DATE, [d/SAP__FI_IFP_GLACCOUNT] = "#") //ENDIF

entry.title: Re: Introduction to the New Framework for Official Document Numbers (ODN)
entry.content: Great post! Thanks a lot!

entry.title: Re: Handling CSRF tokens in SAP Cloud Platform Integration
entry.content: When I am passing the X-CSRF-Token in the headers, in the runtime CPI override the default value(SHA256:551397da7390449ac3d26a5c978db7abe354b36b4050650b3cf7e27e7378ed3a) except the fetched csrf token value(LgG5USLU8D8J0YhHx-toug==). how to stop it??

entry.title: Re: Tree view in RAP
entry.content: Valuable Information, Thank You Very Much

entry.title: Re: Open PO Migration from ECC to S/4 integrated with SCC
entry.content:
Hi;
We had similar scenario with one of our customer so we suggested them to close such POs and create one. As such open POs for customer were less than 100 so the created manually.

I don't knowabout your volume. If it is large maybe you can try it programmatically by downloading PO data from ECC and uploading only valid lines to SAP S/4. Don't forget to close old PO in SAP ECC.

This would be the clean approach.

Thanks
Siddarth

entry.title: Re: SAP SOAP Sender & Receiver Adapters: Your Integration Game Changers
entry.content:
Hi Community,

"

  1. OData responses are typically in JSON or XML with a different format than the SOAP response.
  2. Mapping helps reshape this data into the required SOAP structure.
  • After the SOAP request is received, the OData call fetches additional data (e.g., shipment date) related to the order.
  • The final SOAP response must follow a specific schema or structure defined by the SOAP service (WSDL/XSD)."

Thanks for your selfless sharing, it really help us a lot.

 

🙂

Regards,

ZFiori.
